issues.title: docs: people & money cohesion design spine (#833)
issues.body:
@PipFoweraker -- **squash-merge anytime, zero risk.** DOC-ONLY (no code, no build, non-forking).

The 'people & money' cohesion spine you YES-screamed at: data model + forking split + membership boundary + sequencing + open questions. 3873 words, ASCII-clean. Surfaced real findings worth your eye: **compute_engineers are never billed a salary** anywhere, and payroll is a direct per-turn drain (not routed through the Ledger). Safe to land whenever -- it just parks the design on main.

issues.title: v0.13.1 honesty pass: grey 'coming soon' + right-size hiring verbs
issues.body:
@PipFoweraker -- **SQUASH-MERGE this from your phone whenever.** Safe, non-forking, no rush.

WHAT (the launch-night honesty right-sizes):
- **Advertise a Role** -> greyed 'coming soon' (it spawned random specs; targeted-role hiring is a later patch)
- **Attend Conference** -> greyed 'coming soon' (real form is day-bound / per-tick scheduling)
- **Interview a Candidate / Make an Offer** -> removed from the target-less action bar (the per-candidate hiring panel is the intended path)

SAFE: presentation/flow only. NON-FORKING -- same L2 ladder, zero board impact. Gate green (607 tests, 0 fail). Does NOT touch the v0.13.0 release (that tag is fixed at ae0c2c23).

AFTER YOU MERGE: I bump -> 0.13.1, strip the ~30MB portrait bloat, and cut the player-facing hotpatch build (smaller = faster upload). **Squash-merge = clean.**

issues.title: Revisit ADR-0014 (conferences): days-granularity + travel-cost fine-tuning + conference data -> pdoom-data

labels.name: league:next
labels.description: Waits for the NEXT league rollover -- forking gameplay, batched to the next epoch

issues.body:
Spun out of #789. ADR-0014 ruled the conference/attendance model in MONTHS of time, but the game now runs at DAYS granularity (ADR-0009) -- so the conference mechanics can be honed considerably. Not urgent; captures the design direction.

## Revisit / add
- **Days-granularity pass.** Re-express conference time cost in days, not months.
- **Per-employee travel cost varies** -- by travel distance, and by budget vs business class, etc. More knobs for the player to fine-tune or DELEGATE (foreseen delegation mechanics: an attendee's trip can be a sink on a manager's Attention, not just the founder's).
- **Lateral build-out.** Conferences reuse the SAME scheduling / commitment / Attention / delegation substrate as hiring (#789). Building the pattern laterally (hiring, conferences, papers) is the game's intended "second layer" of depth -- consider whether that substrate itself deserves an ADR.

## Data
- Conferences are real historical events -- cheap to populate. Plan: **populate the game with data NOW** (keep the mechanics tight), and note as TECH DEBT to later re-package + validate + re-ingest via the **pdoom-data** repo (other forks will want this data; it should not live only in-game long-term).
- Keep juicy fictional flavor (see below) OFF real named conferences -- real names = flavor only; scandals ride fictional/generic venues (see caution below).

## Event space this opens (story / antagonists / later alliances + voting)
Travel mishaps + intrigue: missing luggage, delayed flights, tropical diseases, dodgy stopovers, poolside cloaked strangers, etc. Rich vector for introducing antagonists and (later) alliances / meetings / voting mechanics.

CAUTION: attaching fictional scandal (money laundering, shady strangers) to REAL named conferences/people implies real wrongdoing -- keep antagonist events on fictional or generic venues; use real conference names/dates as neutral flavor only.

Tier: next-epoch / later (not the 48h tier).

issues.title: Playtest design ideas: payroll depth, donor base, accounting-software introduces the ledger
issues.body: From live playtest, for workshop #2: (1) PAYROLL DEPTH — reviewing pay/hours is tedious *by design*; make paying payroll cost AP + occasional timesheet-chasing with disorganised scientists, so the player *wants* to automate it via an upgrade. (2) FUNDRAISING -> DONOR BASE — networking-for-rep-to-fundraise could build into a recurring donor base (ties to funding-relationship #395 + ledger receivables). (3) ACCOUNTING-SOFTWARE UPGRADE introduces/gates the Liability Ledger — an old forgotten upgrade idea; diegetically explains how the player 'gets' the ledger, and connects to 'spending buys sight' (buy accounting software = buy financial situational awareness = ledger becomes visible/manageable). Also minor: shooing the office cat gave no doom feedback.

issues.title: design: 'people & money' cohesion spine -- recruitment epic -> roles/salary/manager -> payroll -> finance/roster
issues.body:
Pip 2026-07-24 (design workshop). The recruitment flow is an EPIC whose sub-steps are PER-CANDIDATE, never standalone: advertise-a-ROLE (spawns candidates already APPLYING for that role) -> interview [candidate] -> offer to [candidate] -> onboard -> the hire holds a ROLE (title / manager / monthly SALARY). 'Everyone needs a job' -> salary = a PAYROLL obligation -> lands in the ledger/finance -> surfaced in an expanded Employee screen <-> budget/finance screen.

GOAL: COHESION -- a legible causal chain the player intuits over many interactions ('hired for a role -> it costs salary -> cash tightens -> manage the budget'). METHOD (Pip): forge the CONNECTIONS/data now, surface visuals incrementally in future patches. Inverse of last workshop (which surfaced hidden rule-abstractions); this one connects surfaced pieces into an intuited whole.

FORKING SPLIT (Claude): 
- NON-FORKING / cheap: interview & offer as per-candidate epic sub-steps (UI right-size, ties #830); the DATA FIELDS role/salary/manager/applying-for.
- FORKING / needs balance sweep (epoch/WS-3): advertise-role SPAWNING interested candidates (changes the reachable game); payroll DRAINING money (reshapes the whole difficulty curve = 'every hire is a standing loan', ADR-0003).

OPEN: membership boundary -- WHO is a salaried employee? (NOT the founder = Attention-spender, different class; contractors staff_rider? advisors?). Pin this first.

APPROACH: a data-model DESIGN DOC forges the shape (role/salary/manager/job-interest + hiring->payroll->finance->roster flow) WITHOUT shipping dead fields; code each field when its first consumer (payroll or roster UI) lands. Ties: #574 (payroll/accounting depth), #830 (hiring statefulness), #822 (hire-as-epic), DQ-19 (char/org creation), ADR-0011/0012/0003. Tier: WS-3 / epoch spine.

issues.title: ux/consistency(ledger): express debt due-dates in DAYS/date, not turns (day-precision for sequencing)
issues.body: Pip playtest 2026-07-24: the ledger tracks/shows debts owed in TURNS, which is internally wrong -- the game's canonical clock is DAYS (a 'turn' is just the current plan-month; the sim advances day by day). The ledger should show the actual due DATE (e.g. 'due 16 Aug') plus how far off ('this turn' / 'X days'), so the player can SEQUENCE: a debt due on the 16th lets them reason 'we can pay you next week' and have it be literally TRUE. This is 'coarse hands, fine scoreboard' (ADR-0009) applied to debt -- plan at month grain, debt resolves at day grain, and the gap is where the sequencing drama lives. Verify: does the ledger already store a day/tick fuse (finance_engine fuse_ticks) -> DISPLAY fix; or genuinely track turns -> a bit deeper. Tier: patch:ui / consistency (part of the legibility pass).

issues.title: feat(cold-open): cycle hero backgrounds per text beat
issues.body: Pip (2026-07-24): 'cycle between a couple of hero backgrounds each time the text progresses to add a bit of scene-setting and mood and flavour.' Cold-open currently uses one HERO_ART. Rotate 2-3 bg images as beats advance. Tier: patch:ui / fast-follow.

issues.title: fast-follow(music): drop BPM 10-15%, consider 2 layered tracks
issues.body: Pip (v0.13 playtest 2026-07-24): 'the entire bpm could definitely drop by 10 or 15%, it felt pretty frenetic on the high end.' 'Maybe we have 2 tunes layered over each other?' NOTE: the default music volume was already dropped 50 -> 20 in the v0.13.0 build. BPM / layering is a music-production fast-follow (Fable music session). Tier: patch:ui (non-forking / audio).

issues.title: ux: Settings menu needs a scroll bar
issues.body: Pip playtest 2026-07-24: the Settings menu content overflows -- add a scroll bar / make it scrollable. Tier: patch:ui, quick.

issues.title: ux(hiring): statefulness -- ask WHO, per-candidate interviews, interview popup w/ card+info fade-in, portraits, embedded employee panel
issues.body: Pip playtest 2026-07-24 cluster (extends #789 + #813): (a) 'make an offer' / 'onboard candidate' should explicitly ask WHO if the target candidate isn't inferred from the player starting that person's hiring cycle. (b) Queuing several interviews on turn 1 loses which-candidate statefulness -- each interview should be tied to a specific candidate. (c) On interview, the Operator gets a little popup with the candidate's CARD and previously-hidden info FADES IN (the stateful ?->silhouette->half->full arc, #813). (d) Load candidate IMAGES/portraits into employee cards. (e) The Employee screen could be a SMALL embedded panel in the PLAN section that the player clicks to enlarge, rather than a separate screen to bring up. Tier: mostly WS-3 (forks -- hiring mechanics) + some patch:ui.

issues.title: design(events): default 4 options, grey-out unaffordable (preserved), free outs, Attention-in-corner
issues.body: Pip playtest 2026-07-24 rulings for event popups: (1) DEFAULT to 4 options per event. (2) Unaffordable options render GREYED but stay VISIBLE (preserved event-to-event); mouseover = darker grey, clicks do nothing (the don't-close-explain fix 5f568a9b is the fallback if clicked). (3) If popup events cost Attention by default, make it EXPLICIT in the corner of the popup. (4) 'defer' / 'reject offer' / ignore-style OUTS should be 0 Attention by default (unless a DQ/ADR blocks it). Ties to the cost-display sweep + outs (#825). Tier: patch:ui + a small design ruling.

issues.title: observe/design(ux): hiring actions don't appear in the action queue (top bar + hiring submenu only)
issues.body: Pip (playtest 2026-07-24): hiring actions (interview / offer / onboard) do NOT show up in the bottom-right action queue -- they're tracked only in the top bar of the main UI and inside the hiring submenu. Is this intended (the hiring pipeline has its own dedicated display) or an inconsistency the player trips on (expected them in the queue like other committed actions)? Design call: where should hiring commitments surface? Ties to the action-card-flies-to-queue idea (#819). Tier: patch:ui / design.

issues.title: fix(ux): show option/action costs ON the button + grey out unaffordable
issues.body: Pip (playtest 2026-07-24): costs currently show only OnMouseOver. Represent the cost visually INSIDE the button's clickable area, not just on hover. And grey out the area behind an UNAFFORDABLE cost vs the default texture when it IS affordable -- so the player sees affordability at a glance and does not click a wall. Preventive legibility complement to the don't-close-on-unaffordable fix (branch legibility-affordance). Tier: patch:ui, hotpatch/fast-follow.

issues.title: feat(mechanic): overbook TEETH -- forcing-function instead of a flat reject
issues.body: Pip (2026-07-24): 'we get to instantiate TEETH!' Right now overbooking Attention is a flat reject (soft-lock now guarded). The DESIGN opportunity: make overbooking a real forced DECISION -- (i) cancel a queued item (reuse the existing MonthPlan.pay_by_cannibalizing LIFO-cancel, month_plan.gd:134-151) or (ii) accept it at a cost (technical debt / reputation / doom tax). Hook: the over-budget branch in game_manager.select_action (game_manager.gd:172-191); surface via a MonthPlan 'over-budget, here is what it would cost' result that select_action turns into a UI decision, mirroring MonthController.resolve_current_window. Changes gameplay -> forks the ladder. Tier: league:next / WS-3 material.

issues.title: docs: rendered HTML architecture + nomenclature map (turn/game systems, with diagrams)
issues.body:
Pip (2026-07-24, lightly-tasked do-later): the turn + game-system architecture + nomenclature is getting hard to hold in one's head. Want a nicely-rendered INTERNAL HTML page WITH DIAGRAMS that lays it all out in one place.

Should cover:
- **The turn/month structure**: plan-month (queue actions, spend Attention from the 20 pool) -> day-tick playout via MonthController -> response windows (pause-on-window) -> month review. The plan-time (sorcery) vs interrupt-time (instant) split (ADR-0009).
- **The core loop** and how the pieces connect (GameManager / TurnManager / MonthController / MonthPlan / GameState / EventService / WindowResolver / FinanceEngine / doom_system / Ledger).
- **The NOMENCLATURE glossary** (Pip is actively memorising these): task, handle, event, action, option, window, response VERBS (handle_reserve / handle_cannibalize / defer / ignore), event-option ids (build_upon / acknowledge / ...), Attention / reserve / cannibalize, epoch / league / seed / board / ladder_version, ledger / liability, etc.

Style: same treatment as docs/RELEASE_AND_LEAGUE_CYCLE.html (theme-aware light/dark, ASCII-only, self-contained single file). Diagrams via inline SVG or bundled mermaid (note: a local double-click file can't fetch mermaid from a CDN -- either inline the lib or hand-author SVG). 

Tier: docs / fast-follow, do-later.
